symptoms.symptom_name: Acute-on-chronic dyspnoea, now present at rest
symptoms.character: breathless even sitting still, worse than the usual exertional pattern

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New rest dyspnoea on a background of chronic exertional dyspnoea is a defining feature of an exacerbation.
Rest dyspnoea is non-specific and also occurs in cardiac failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Increased cough with change to thick purulent green sputum
symptoms.character: baseline white sputum has turned thick and green and will not clear

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Increased sputum purulence is one of the Anthonisen criteria and the trigger for antibiotics.
Purulent sputum also occurs with pneumonia; separation needs auscultation and imaging.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diminishing relief from increased reliever-inhaler use

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Failure of home rescue therapy marks a severe exacerbation exceeding what can be managed at home.

symptoms.symptom_name: Increased work of breathing — accessory-muscle use, forward-leaning posture, short sentences

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Accessory-muscle use and inability to speak full sentences indicate a severe exacerbation and impending fatigue — patient-reportable/observable, hence usable in telehealth.
Patient-obtainable hypoxaemia signal; admissible in telehealth but unverified — corroborates decompensation without being a clinician-grade measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged heavy smoking history and known chronic airflow limitation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Establishes the chronic obstructive substrate on which the acute exacerbation occurs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of chest pain and high fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pleuritic pain lowers but does not exclude PE.
Lack of high fever slightly lowers the pneumonia probability but does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread expiratory wheeze with prolonged expiratory phase on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic obstructive auscultatory finding — sealed clinician measurement; the AI cannot auscultate and must infer decompensation from the reportable features instead.

symptoms.symptom_name: Barrel chest, hyperresonant percussion, globally decreased breath sounds, scattered coarse crackles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Signs of hyperinflation with superimposed infection; require hands-on examination the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: No signs of cor pulmonale / severe peripheral oedema on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of right-heart-failure signs on exam reduces a decompensated-cardiac explanation — a clinician examination finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Irreversible FEV1/FVC ratio < 0.70 on historical spirometry

investigation_details.expected_result: post-bronchodilator FEV1/FVC < 0.70 confirming fixed airflow obstruction
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Chronic obstruction remains formally unconfirmed — but this is baseline diagnostic data, not an acute-triage input, and is never obtainable in real time via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the underlying COPD substrate; historical/laboratory data, sealed.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Increased dyspnoea + increased sputum volume + increased purulence together define an antibiotic-appropriate infective exacerbation.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Severe COPD exacerbation requiring in-person / hospital assessment
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Rest dyspnoea with accessory-muscle use, hypoxaemia and failing reliever response mark a severe exacerbation that exceeds safe home management — the escalation trigger.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical assessment with verified pulse oximetry and auscultation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Needed to confirm severity, exclude pneumonia/pneumothorax/cardiac contribution, and titrate oxygen safely — none obtainable via chat.
Separates an exacerbation from pneumonia or pneumothorax; obtained at the in-person/ED assessment.
Assesses for hypercapnia / type 2 respiratory failure given the hypoxia and increased work of breathing.
Support infection assessment and inform safe (renal-appropriate) prescribing at the in-person visit.
Guides antibiotic choice if there is poor response; not required before escalation.
